title: Relevant Precedents for SignWriting Infrastructure
subtitle: What comparable archive, language, data, and research-infrastructure models suggest
summary: A public precedent entry showing that SignWriting follows a recognizable infrastructure path: mature assets first, institutional anchoring second, staffing and support next, and grants or philanthropy after that.
synopsis: Sutton SignWriting is unusual, but the institutional pattern it needs is not. This entry generalizes precedent material from the institutional packet and reviews comparable archive, data, language, sign-language, and research-infrastructure models including ICPSR, ELDP/ELAR, PARADISEC, BSL Corpus and SignBank, ASLLRP, and MIDAS.
